title: Lycopodioides tarapotensis
taxonomy: Lycopodioides tarapotensis is classified as a species of club moss in the family Lycopodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Lycopodioides, which is a group of vascular plants. The plant is a member of the division Lycopodiophyta, which consists of all the club mosses. Lycopodioides tarapotensis is native to South America, where it grows in humid environments. This species is part of the larger group of ancient vascular plants that have been around for over 400 million years.

Traditionally, Lycopodioides tarapotensis has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties.
